title: Nepal FM visits India to deepen bilateral cooperation across multiple sectors
lede: Nepal's Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal visited India on 5-6 June 2026 for bilateral talks with External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar, reviewing the full spectrum of India-Nepal relations and welcoming a new mutual legal assistance agreement.

sections.context: Nepal's Foreign Minister H.E. Mr. Shisir Khanal undertook an official visit to India starting 5 June 2026 at the invitation of External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar, signaling continued diplomatic engagement between the two neighbouring countries. The bilateral talks held in New Delhi on 6 June 2026 covered a comprehensive review of India-Nepal relations spanning development cooperation, connectivity, trade and transit, energy, and people-to-people ties. Both Ministers expressed satisfaction with progress achieved in bilateral cooperation and welcomed recent initiatives to enhance cooperation in innovation and startups, digital and financial technology, and training and capacity building. The visit underscores the commitment of both nations to intensify efforts towards enhancing their multifaceted partnership.
sections.threads: The visit coincides with broader South Asian diplomatic engagement patterns, where neighbouring countries conduct regular high-level exchanges to address connectivity and economic cooperation challenges. The focus on trade and transit cooperation reflects persistent regional infrastructure priorities, while discussions on energy suggest ongoing efforts to address bilateral resource-sharing frameworks. The ministers' exchange of views on regional and multilateral issues of mutual interest indicates that India-Nepal cooperation extends beyond bilateral matters to encompass South Asian geopolitical considerations and multilateral forum coordination.
sections.signal: The completion of internal processes for entry into force of the India-Nepal Mutual Legal Assistance Agreement in Criminal Matters (MLAA) represents a significant institutional development. Analysts should monitor the formal entry into force of this Agreement and its practical implementation in cross-border criminal investigations and legal cooperation. The emphasis on innovation, startups, digital technology, and capacity building suggests the bilateral agenda is expanding into emerging sectors; tracking new initiatives in these areas will indicate whether the partnership is modernizing beyond traditional development cooperation frameworks.

links.label: India-Nepal Mutual Legal Assistance Agreement in Criminal Matters
links.preview: The MLAA has completed internal processes for entry into force, promising enhanced institutional legal frameworks for criminal cooperation.

links.label: India-Nepal connectivity and transit cooperation
links.preview: Trade and transit cooperation remains a core component of bilateral discussions alongside development and energy partnerships.

links.label: South Asian digital and fintech cooperation initiatives
links.preview: Both nations are expanding cooperation into digital and financial technology as part of modernizing their partnership.

links.label: India-Nepal energy cooperation agreements
links.preview: Energy remains a key bilateral sector under ongoing review and development in the India-Nepal partnership.

links.label: Nepal capacity building and training initiatives
links.preview: Training and capacity building have been identified as areas for intensified cooperation between India and Nepal.
